prevalence.trend: increasing with urbanization, diet shifts, and obesity

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated via cardiovascular and renal complications

vulnerablePopulations: South Asian phenotype (higher risk at lower BMI)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: FPG ≥126 mg/dL, HbA1c ≥6.5%, 2h OGTT ≥200 mg/dL, or random glucose ≥200 mg/dL with symptoms; per ADA/ICMR-aligned criteria

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: opportunistic and programmatic screening under NPCDCS; heterogeneous by state
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; large diagnosis and follow-up gaps outside urban centers

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Screening via NPCDCS in primary care and camps; HbA1c testing availability varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle + metformin; sulfonylureas and insulin widely used; SGLT2i/GLP-1RA increasing in urban private care; Ayurveda often used as adjunct (e.g., diet/yoga, herbal products)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High out-of-pocket costs, variable continuity of care, and delayed complication screening
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Large-scale NCD programs and broad access to affordable generic diabetes medications
